name: owned_threads_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_4_owned_threads_shared_across_containers(
    pm: ProcessManager,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c1),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        pm.get_container(c1).owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        pm.get_container(c2).owned_threads@.contains(t)
        || !pm.get_container(c2).owned_threads@.contains(t),
{
}
reason: Neither container_perms_wf nor container_tree_wf constrains owned_threads to be disjoint across containers — the same thread pointer can appear in multiple containers' owned_threads Ghost sets

name: memory_disjoint_container_not_proc
code:
proof fn phi_5_memory_disjoint_container_not_proc(
    pm: ProcessManager,
    ptr: usize,
)
    requires
        pm.memory_disjoint(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(ptr),
    ensures
        !pm.proc_dom().contains(ptr),
        !pm.thread_dom().contains(ptr),
        !pm.endpoint_dom().contains(ptr),
{
}
reason: memory_disjoint uses Set::disjoint which is defined extensionally — since ContainerPtr/ProcPtr/ThreadPtr/EndpointPtr are all type aliases for usize, the disjointness constraint prevents ANY pointer value from serving dual roles, but this is only enforced at the Ghost level with no runtime check
